reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Code reading is accurate: ComputeCells returns cells without explicit length validation, and the loop accesses cells[0..127] assuming len(cells)==128. However, this is not a vulnerability. The c-kzg-4844 library (v2.1.1) is the canonical reference implementation of the Ethereum consensus spec, which explicitly requires compute_cells to return exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB (128) cells as a postcondition (see SG-018 INV). The library has been battle-tested in production across all Ethereum clients since Deneb/EIP-4844. No evidence of version mismatch or library bug causing fewer cells. If the library violated this guarantee, it would fail the extensive test suites in ethereum/consensus-specs. This is a defense-in-depth observation (explicit validation would be safer) rather than an exploitable vulnerability. Spec reference: SG-018 invariant 'Output contains exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells'. Severity: Informational per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE (no direct security risk, library behavior is trusted and verified).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: SG-018 (compute_cells): INV: Output contains exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells
